I have all the info I need. The reverse_shell function:
- creates AF_INET socket
- connects to 192.168.1.100 (at 0x47a010) on port 0x115c (htons) = 4444
- dup2 stdin/stdout/stderr to socket
- execve("/bin/sh", 0, 0) (at 0x47a01e)
- main forks, child sleeps then runs reverse_shell, parent runs fork_bomb (infinite fork loop)
